recipes.title: Ultimate Bolognese Sauce
recipes.description: A refined, professional-standard ragù using precision temperature control to emulsify fats and tenderise protein over a long, controlled simmer.
instructions: Add the pancetta to the Ninja CI100UK food processor attachment and blend until finely chopped.

Place the Lodge L8DD3 Cast Iron Double Dutch Oven on the Sage The Control Freak. Set the pan temperature to 110°C. Add 30ml of the extra virgin olive oil and the blended pancetta. Let it sweat gently, then add the chopped onion, celery, and carrots.
Cook the soffritto at 110°C with the lid on for 10 minutes. Every 2 minutes, check and stir to prevent any colouration using a wooden spoon.
After 7 minutes, or once the vegetables are tender and glossy, add a splash of the wine, replace the lid, and finish the cooking time.

Drizzle in the remaining extra virgin olive oil and increase the Sage Control Freak pan temperature to 210°C. Add the minced beef and pork, using a wooden spoon to break up the meat thoroughly. Mix until well incorporated into the soffritto.
Once the meat has lost its raw red colour, pour in the remaining wine and mix well.
Let it simmer for 10 minutes then season with a generous amount of salt and pepper.
Stir in the tomato purée until evenly distributed, then add the passata and canned peeled tomatoes.

Cover with a lid. Insert the Sage Control Freak probe into the sauce. Set the probe temperature to 85°C–92°C with 'Slow' or 'Medium' intensity. Cook for 3 hours, stirring every 30 minutes to ensure nothing sticks to the base.
Remove the lid, stir the sauce well, and continue cooking uncovered at the same probe temperature (85°C–92°C) for 1 more hour.
Taste and adjust the salt and pepper as needed, scraping the bottom of the pot as you stir. Ensure the heat remains low.
For a silkier texture, stir in the full cream milk and cook for a final 5 minutes.
equipmentNeeded: Sage The Control Freak induction hob
Lodge L8DD3 Cast Iron Double Dutch Oven

technicalWarnings: Ensure the Control Freak is set to pan temperature for this stage to precisely soften the soffritto without browning.
The 210°C pan temperature is required for the Maillard reaction; work the meat quickly with the spoon to prevent large clumps.
Using the probe to maintain 85°C–92°C prevents the sauce from reaching a rolling boil, which would split the fats and toughen the meat.

history.changeDescription: Set soffritto sweating temperature to 110°C.; Set meat browning temperature to 210°C.; Establish a simmer range of 85°C-92°C using the temperature probe.

snapshot.title: Ultimate Bolognese Sauce
snapshot.description: A traditional, slow-cooked Bolognese combining minced beef, pork, and pancetta with a classic soffritto and tomatoes, developed over four hours for deep flavour and a silky finish.
instructions: Add pancetta to the Ninja CI100UK food processor attachment and blend until finely chopped.

In the Lodge L8DD3 Cast Iron Double Dutch Oven over low-medium heat (using the Sage The Control Freak), add 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil and the blended pancetta. Let it sweat gently, then add the chopped onion, celery, and carrots.
Cook the soffritto with the lid on for about 10 minutes. Every 2 minutes, check and stir to prevent burning using a wooden spoon.
After about 7 minutes, or when all ingredients are tender and glossy, add a splash of the wine, close the lid again, and let it finish cooking.
Drizzle in a little more extra virgin olive oil, then add the minced beef and pork, using a wooden spoon to break up the meat really well, and mix it into the soffritto until well incorporated.
Once the meat turns grey, pour in the remaining wine and mix well.
Let it simmer for 10 minutes then season with a generous amount of salt and pepper.
Next, stir in the tomato purée and mix until it’s spread throughout the meat, then add the passata and canned peeled tomatoes.
Cover with a lid and cook on low heat for 3 hours. Every 30 minutes, check the sauce, give it a stir, and make sure nothing is sticking to the bottom.
After 3 hours, remove the lid, stir the sauce well and continue cooking uncovered for 1 more hour.
Add more salt and pepper to adjust the seasoning (after you taste it), and scrape the bottom of the pot as you stir. Keep the heat low.
If you want a silkier sauce, add a splash of full cream milk and let it cook for another 5 minutes. This step is optional, but highly recommended.
equipmentNeeded: Lodge L8DD3 Cast Iron Double Dutch Oven

technicalWarnings: Ensure the lid is secure to keep moisture trapped; check every 2 minutes.
The slow cook is essential; monitor every 30 minutes to prevent catching on the base.
Reducing uncovered in the final hour is key for the correct thickness.

recipes.description: A classic high-hydration dough designed for a long cold-ferment, resulting in a superior crust texture and flavour.
instructions: Place the flour, salt and yeast in the bowl of the Kenwood Chef Elite. Using the dough hook, mix to combine.
Add the water and knead on low speed for 5 minutes, or until the dough is smooth and elastic.

Transfer the dough to a large bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and allow to proof in a warm, draft-free place for 2 hours, or until doubled in size. If using the Anova Culinary Precision Oven, select the proof function, set to 30°C and proof for 1 hour.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured benchtop and divide into 4 even pieces, approximately 240g each. Shape each piece into a smooth ball.

Sprinkle a proofing box or deep sided container with semolina. Place the dough balls in the box, leaving 10cm between them. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 6 hours or up to 5 days.
An hour before cooking in the Sage The Smart Oven Pizzaiolo, remove the dough from the refrigerator.
recipes.title: Essential Neapolitan-Style Pizza Dough

technicalWarnings: Ensure the dough is smooth and elastic before stopping the mixer.
Ensure significant spacing between balls to allow for expansion during the cold ferment.

ingredients.raw: 570g bread flour - plus extra for dusting
ingredients.ingredientName: bread flour - plus extra for dusting

instructions: Pulse the biscuits in the Magimix Cook Expert until they form a fine crumb. Melt the butter in a Judge Vista saucepan on the Control Freak set to 60°C. Combine the two, press firmly into the base of a lined 15cm or 18cm springform tin, and chill in the fridge for 20 minutes.
Fit the Kenwood Chef Elite with the K-beater. Combine the cream cheese and sugar, beating on the lowest speed until just smooth.
Add the eggs one at a time to the cream cheese mixture, followed by the lime zest, lime juice, and double cream. Mix on low speed until just combined.
Preheat the Anova Precision Oven to 120°C with 100% Steam.
Pour the filling over the chilled biscuit base. Place the tin on the wire rack in the centre of the oven. Bake until the internal temperature reaches 66°C as measured by the ThermoPro Lightning.

Turn off the oven and crack the door open. Let the cheesecake sit inside for 30 minutes to temper. Remove and allow to reach room temperature before chilling in the fridge for at least 6 hours, or overnight, for a final set.

technicalWarnings: Do not overwork the mixture; incorporating air will cause the cheesecake to rise and then crack during service.
The edges should be set but the centre must remain slightly wobbly at the 66°C pull-point.

recipes.description: A sharp, vibrant citrus cheesecake utilizing 100% steam to achieve a silky, crack-free texture without the need for a traditional water bath.
recipes.title: Precision Baked Lime Cheesecake

ingredients: 300g mozzarella (Fior di Latte or low-moisture), drained
snapshot.title: Neapolitan Pepperoni Pizza
instructions: Prep the Sauce: Combine the San Marzano tomatoes and sea salt in a jug. Pulse with the Bamix multipurpose blade 3 to 4 times to maintain texture. Do not over-process into a purée.
Prep the Toppings: Use the Benriner mandoline to slice the pepperoni into 1.5mm rounds. Ensure the mozzarella is thoroughly drained and patted dry to prevent a soggy base.
Preheat the Oven: Set the Sage Pizzaiolo to 400°C on the 'Wood Fired' setting. Allow the stone deck to saturate for at least 20 minutes, confirming the temperature with the ThermoPro.

Stretch the Dough: Dust the work surface with flour or semolina. Working with one dough ball at a time, press from the centre outwards to create a 2cm rim (cornicione). Stretch gently to a 25–30cm diameter.
Dress the Pizza: Apply 60–80ml of the prepared sauce to the centre and spiral outwards. Add a third of the parmesan, followed by a third of the mozzarella and pepperoni.
Cook: Transfer the pizza to the peel and launch into the Pizzaiolo. Bake for 90 to 120 seconds until the crust shows leopard spotting and the cheese is bubbling.
Service and Recovery: Remove the pizza for service. Allow the oven 2 minutes to recover its deck temperature—monitored by the ThermoPro—before launching the next pizza.
snapshot.description: A professional three-serving Neapolitan service focusing on high-heat blistered crusts and precise topping preparation using the Sage Pizzaiolo.

technicalWarnings: Do not over-blend the tomatoes; if they become aerated, the sauce will turn orange and lose its fresh character.
The mandoline blades are professional grade; always use the guard when slicing the pepperoni.
The stone deck must be fully saturated with heat to achieve the required base crispness.
Do not leave the oven unattended; at 400°C, the window between a perfect bake and a burnt crust is only a few seconds.

history.changeDescription: Updated pepperoni specification from whole stick to pre-sliced.; Adjusted assembly to place pepperoni on top of the mozzarella for better texture.; Strategic placement of thin pepperoni slices toward the centre of the pizza.

instructions: Prep the Sauce: Combine the San Marzano tomatoes and sea salt in a jug. Pulse with the Bamix multipurpose blade 3 to 4 times to maintain texture. Do not over-process into a purée.
Prep the Toppings: Ensure the mozzarella is thoroughly drained and patted dry to prevent a soggy base. Set aside the pre-sliced pepperoni.
Preheat the Oven: Set the Sage Pizzaiolo to 400°C on the 'Wood Fired' setting. Allow the stone deck to saturate for at least 20 minutes, confirming the temperature with the ThermoPro.

Stretch the Dough: Dust the work surface with flour or semolina. Working with one dough ball at a time, press from the centre outwards to create a 2cm rim (cornicione). Stretch gently to a 25–30cm diameter.

Dress the Pizza: Apply 60–80ml of the prepared sauce to the centre and spiral outwards. Add a third of the parmesan, followed by a third of the mozzarella. Place the pepperoni slices flat and non-overlapping on top of the cheese, positioning them slightly away from the edges.
Cook: Transfer the pizza to the peel and launch into the Pizzaiolo. Bake for 90 to 120 seconds until the crust shows leopard spotting and the cheese is bubbling.
Service and Recovery: Remove the pizza for service. Allow the oven 2 minutes to recover its deck temperature—monitored by the ThermoPro—before launching the next pizza.
recipes.description: A classic high-heat pizza featuring pre-sliced pepperoni arranged to render perfectly without charring in the intense heat of the Sage Pizzaiolo.

technicalWarnings: Over-processing the tomatoes will result in a watery sauce; keep pulses short to maintain pulp texture.
Ensure pepperoni slices do not overlap; this allows the fat to render properly and prevents the meat from steaming.
Monitor the stone temperature closely; launching a second pizza before the deck recovers will result in a pale, undercooked base.

recipes.title: Neapolitan Pepperoni Pizza

ingredients.raw: 300g mozzarella (Fior di Latte or low-moisture), drained

recipes.title: Classic Precision Carbonara
recipes.description: A Roman staple executed with technical accuracy, using the Sage Control Freak to maintain a perfect 55°C emulsion for a silky, stable sauce.

technicalWarnings: Ensure the Control Freak probe is active; 55°C is the critical threshold for a stable emulsion.
Agitation is key here; do not stop tossing the pasta until the sauce has fully emulsified and thickened.

instructions: Fill the Judge Vista saucepan with water, add a generous pinch of salt, and bring to a rolling boil.
Place the guanciale into the cold De Buyer 28cm frying pan. Bring to a medium heat and render until the fat is transparent and the pork is crisp.
In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolks, whole egg, and grated Pecorino Romano together until a thick, uniform paste forms.
Add the crushed peppercorns to the frying pan with the guanciale for the final 30 seconds of rendering to toast the spice.
Boil the spaghetti until it is approximately 2 minutes away from being al dente.
Remove 60ml of the starchy pasta water and slowly whisk it into the egg and cheese mixture to temper the proteins.
Set the Sage Control Freak to 55°C and place the De Buyer pan onto the induction surface.
Use tongs to transfer the pasta directly from the water into the frying pan, allowing some residual moisture to carry over.
Pour the tempered egg mixture over the pasta. Toss and stir vigorously, allowing the Control Freak to maintain the precise heat required for the emulsion to thicken without scrambling.
Adjust the sauce consistency with a splash more pasta water if required until the strands are glossily coated; serve immediately on warmed plates.
